title: GameStop Q4 Earnings: Beat Expectations, Collectibles Show Growth
text: GameStop reported fourth-quarter fiscal 2024 results that exceeded profit forecasts while revenue fell short. The company posted adjusted earnings per share of 30 cents, surpassing the consensus estimate of 9 cents and improving from 22 cents in the prior year. However, net sales reached $1.282 billion, missing the $1.450 billion consensus and dropping 28.5% from $1.794 billion previously. Hardware and accessories declined 33.7% to $725.8 million. Software fell 38.5% to $286.2 million. Collectibles increased 15.8% to $270.6 million, demonstrating strength in this category. Despite lower absolute sales, the company achieved operational efficiency gains. Gross margin expanded 490 basis points to 28.3%, while adjusted operating margin improved 320 basis points to 6.6%. Adjusted EBITDA climbed 9.7% to $96.5 million. GameStop maintained a robust financial position with $4.76 billion in cash, generated $158.8 million in free cash flow during the quarter, and reduced inventory to $480.2 million from $632.5 million year-over-year.
